title: 10 best herbal teas for excessive sweating
intro_desc: Herbal teas for excessive sweating are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices that can help regulate perspiration. These teas are caffeine-free and often contain natural ingredients known for their calming, diuretic, or antiperspirant properties. Examples include chamomile, known for its calming effects; sage, which has antiperspirant properties; and peppermint, which can help regulate body temperature. By drinking these teas regularly, individuals may experience a reduction in excessive sweating, leading to improved confidence, comfort, and overall quality of life. For instance, wearing certain clothes or participating in social activities without the fear of sweating excessively can significantly boost one's self-esteem and well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with excessive sweating because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help regulate sweat glands and reduce perspiration. The herb, also known as Achillea millefolium, has been used traditionally for its healing properties and is believed to tighten pores and improve skin health. Yarrow tea is thought to stimulate the production of sweat that is high in toxins, while reducing the overall amount of sweat produced. Additionally, yarrow tea is known to have calming effects on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress-induced sweating. Drinking yarrow tea regularly can help manage excessive sweating and promote overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Yarrow tea contains tannins that help to tighten skin pores and reduce sweat production, providing a temporary solution for excessive sweating.
Antiperspirant properties: The flavonoids present in yarrow tea have been shown to reduce sweat production by inhibiting the activity of sweat glands.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tea contains sesquiterpene lactones that have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation caused by excessive sweating.
Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow tea has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of volatile oils, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause body odor.

Diaphoretic properties: Yarrow tea contains lactones that stimulate perspiration, which may seem counterintuitive for excessive sweating. However, this property can help to detoxify the body and regulate body temperature, reducing the likelihood of excessive sweating caused by overheating.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Crush the herbs to release their oils, then place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the infuser or strain the tea to remove the herbs.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, to help reduce excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain azulene, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate sweat glands.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids that have astringent properties, helping to reduce excessive sweating.
Stems: Contain tannins that can help constrict sweat glands and reduce perspiration.
Roots: Have been used traditionally to treat excessive sweating due to their astringent and styptic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with excessive sweating because it contains compounds that have antiperspirant and astringent properties. These properties help to tighten pores and reduce the production of sweat. Wormwood tea is also known for its calming and relaxing effects, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, two common triggers of excessive sweating. Additionally, wormwood tea has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help to soothe and protect the skin from irritation and infection, which can occur as a result of excessive sweating. Drinking wormwood tea regularly can help to regulate sweat production, promote skin health, and reduce the occurrence of excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Anti-perspirant properties: Wormwood tea contains tannins that help to constrict sweat glands, reducing the amount of sweat produced.
Astringent properties: The astringent properties of wormwood tea, due to its tannin content, help to dry up excess sweat on the skin.
Antimicrobial properties: The volatile oils in wormwood tea, including thujone, have antimicrobial effects that can help prevent body odor caused by excessive sweating.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation caused by excessive sweating.

Nervine properties: Wormwood tea has nervine properties, which can help regulate the nervous system and reduce anxiety or stress-induced sweating. This is attributed to the presence of sedative alkaloids like lactucin and lactucopicrin.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried wormwood herb to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the herbs.
Drink the tea up to three times a day, between meals, for excessive sweating relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Wormwood leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for excessive sweating due to their high concentration of tannins, which can help tighten skin pores and reduce perspiration.
Flowers: Wormwood flowers are used in teas for excessive sweating due to their calming properties, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress, both of which can contribute to excessive perspiration.
Seeds: Wormwood seeds are often used in medicinal teas for excessive sweating because they contain thujone, a compound that can help regulate body temperature and reduce sweating.
Bark: Wormwood bark is used in teas for excessive sweating due to its bitter taste, which can help stimulate digestion and reduce the production of sweat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch tea helps with excessive sweating because it contains compounds called tannins and flavonoids, which have astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the skin, thereby reducing the production of sweat. Additionally, silver birch has natural diuretic properties, which can help eliminate excess fluids from the body and reduce the amount of sweat produced. Furthermore, the anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties of silver birch tea can help soothe and cleanse the skin, reducing the risk of skin irritation and body odor associated with excessive sweating. Drinking silver birch tea regularly can help regulate sweat production and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Silver birch tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can be important for excessive sweating as it can help alleviate any skin irritation or discomfort caused by sweating. This is attributed to the betulinic acid and other triterpenes present in silver birch.

Astringent: The astringent properties of silver birch tea help to tighten and constrict body tissues, which can be beneficial for excessive sweating as it can help reduce the production of sweat and provide a temporary relief. This is due to the tannins present in silver birch.

Diaphoretic: Silver birch tea has diaphoretic properties, which means it can promote sweating. This might seem counterintuitive for excessive sweating, but it can help the body to eliminate toxins and can also help regulate body temperature. This is due to the essential oils and flavonoids present in silver birch.

Antimicrobial: Silver birch tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin. This is important for excessive sweating as it can help prevent body odor and skin infections. This is due to the betulinic acid and other triterpenes present in silver birch.

Diuretic: Silver birch tea has diuretic properties, which means it can help to increase urine production. This can be beneficial for excessive sweating as it can help the body to eliminate excess fluid and toxins. This is due to the flavonoids and essential oils present in silver birch.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried silver birch leaves with 8 ounces of cold water.
Slowly bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat.
Once boiling, reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool down for a few minutes.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day for best results.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the silver birch plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for excessive sweating due to their astringent properties, which help to reduce sweating.
Bark: The bark of the silver birch plant contains tannins, which have a drying effect on the skin and can help to reduce excessive sweating.
Buds: The buds of the silver birch plant are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help to regulate sweat production.
Roots: The roots of the silver birch plant contain substances that help to narrow the blood vessels and reduce perspiration.
Seeds: The seeds of the silver birch plant contain essential oils that have a calming effect on the body and can help to regulate the activity of the sweat glands.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with excessive sweating because it contains high levels of antioxidants and tannins, which can help to reduce sweat production. These compounds work by constricting the sweat glands and reducing overall body moisture. Additionally, white tea has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to soothe and tighten the skin, reducing the appearance of sweat marks. Furthermore, white tea has a cooling effect on the body, which can help to regulate body temperature and reduce the likelihood of excessive sweating. Overall, white tea is a natural and effective way to help manage excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Antiperspirant properties: White tea contains tannins and catechins, which have been shown to reduce sweat production by constricting sweat glands.
Antibacterial properties: The presence of polyphenols in white tea helps inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for body odor, making it effective in controlling excessive sweating and body odor.
Astringent properties: White tea contains high levels of tannins, which have astringent properties that can help tighten skin pores and reduce excessive sweating.

Anti-inflammatory properties: White tea's antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness and discomfort caused by excessive sweating.
Diuretic properties: White tea's diuretic properties, due to its caffeine content, can help eliminate excess fluids from the body, indirectly reducing the amount of sweat produced.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water and let it cool down to 160-185°F (71-85°C), as boiling water can damage the delicate white tea leaves.
Add one teaspoon (2-3 grams) of dried organic white tea leaves to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the heated water over the tea leaves and let them steep for 1-5 minutes, depending on how strong you prefer the taste.
While steeping, prepare a separate pot or container with cold water and ice cubes.

After steeping, strain the tea into a glass or cup, then mix it with an equal amount of cold water to reduce the concentration and cool it down. Enjoy the refreshing herbal white tea, which may help regulate excessive sweating due to its natural antioxidants and mild diuretic properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: White tea leaves are rich in antioxidants and tannins, which can help reduce sweat production and have astringent properties.
Buds: The young buds of the white tea plant contain high levels of catechins, which can help regulate sweat glands and reduce excessive sweating.
Stems: White tea stems contain flavonoids and polyphenols, which can help balance the body's temperature and reduce excessive perspiration.
Roots: Although not as commonly used as the leaves, stems, and buds, white tea roots contain active compounds that can help control sweat gland activity and reduce excessive sweating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roman chamomile tea helps with excessive sweating because it contains active compounds like apigenin and bisabolol, which have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties. These compounds can help regulate the activity of sweat glands and reduce excessive sweating. Moreover, chamomile tea has been used traditionally as a natural remedy for calming the nervous system, which can also help control stress-induced sweating. Its soothing effects on the mind and body make it an ideal beverage for those looking for a natural and caffeine-free way to manage excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Roman chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to active constituents like bisabolol and chamazulene, can help alleviate inflammation in the sweat glands, reducing excessive sweating.
Antiperspirant: The astringent qualities of Roman chamomile tea, caused by tannins, help constrict sweat glands, reducing the amount of sweat produced.
Anxiolytic: Roman chamomile tea's anxiolytic effects, from flavonoids like apigenin, can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can be a cause of excessive sweating.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of Roman chamomile tea, due to active constituents like matricin, can help alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with excessive sweating.
Sedative: Roman chamomile tea's sedative effects, from apigenin and other volatile oils, can promote better sleep, which can help regulate the body's sweat response.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for a minute or two, so it's not boiling hot when you add the chamomile.
Pour the nearly boiling water over 2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers and let it steep, covered, for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the chamomile flowers, leaving only the liquid.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in cooling and drying properties, chamomile flowers are commonly used to make medicinal teas for excessive sweating.
Leaves: Containing tannins that reduce sweating, chamomile leaves are a great addition to medicinal teas for managing excessive sweating.
Stems: Packed with soothing compounds, chamomile stems help alleviate symptoms of excessive sweating when used in medicinal teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka tea helps with excessive sweating because it contains natural ingredients like sage, chamomile, and yarrow, which have astringent properties that can help tighten pores and reduce sweat production. These herbs also have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the likelihood of body odor. Additionally, the tea's natural antioxidants can help support overall health and well-being, which can contribute to a balanced and regulated sweat response. By incorporating Eureka tea into your daily routine, you may be able to find relief from excessive sweating and feel more confident and comfortable in your skin.
remedy_properties: Antiperspirant properties: Eureka tea contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help constrict sweat glands and reduce perspiration.

Antimicrobial properties: Eureka tea has antimicrobial compounds, such as catechins and flavonoids, that can help control bacteria growth on the skin and reduce body odor associated with excessive sweating.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Eureka tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as quercetin, that can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin caused by excessive sweating.
Antioxidant properties: Eureka tea is rich in antioxidants, such as polyphenols, that can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive sweating.
Diaphoretic properties: Eureka tea has diaphoretic properties, which can help stimulate sweating and promote detoxification, while regulating body temperature and reducing excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried yarrow leaves, 1 tablespoon of dried sage leaves, and 2 cups of water, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat, let it simmer for 15 minutes, and allow the herbs to steep.
Strain the tea to remove the solid particles, then compost the used herbs.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day, for best results in managing excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, the leaves of the eureka plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for excessive sweating due to their astringent properties that help to reduce perspiration.

Roots: Containing high amounts of alkaloids and saponins, the roots of the eureka plant are used in medicinal teas for excessive sweating as they help to balance the body's fluid levels and regulate sweat gland activity.

Flowers: Eureka plant's flowers are a great source of essential oils and polyphenols, making them an excellent addition to medicinal teas for excessive sweating as they have a calming effect on the nervous system and help to reduce stress-induced sweating.

Seeds: The seeds of the eureka plant are rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal teas for excessive sweating as they help to nourish and protect the skin, reducing the likelihood of skin irritations caused by excessive sweating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with excessive sweating because it contains high levels of silica, a mineral that supports collagen production and strengthens connective tissues. This can help reduce excessive perspiration by improving the overall health and functioning of the skin, which is responsible for regulating body temperature and releasing sweat. Additionally, horsetail tea has astringent properties that can help tighten pores and reduce the amount of sweat released by the skin. Drinking horsetail tea regularly may help reduce excessive sweating and improve overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Horsetail tea's astringent properties help to tighten the skin and sweat glands, reducing excessive sweating. This is due to the presence of tannins and silicic acid.
Diuretic: Horsetail tea promotes diuresis, helping the body eliminate excess fluid and reducing sweating. This is attributed to the high potassium and flavonoid content.
Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea reduces inflammation, which can help alleviate irritation and discomfort caused by excessive sweating. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antiperspirant: Horsetail tea has antiperspirant properties, helping to directly reduce sweating. This is attributed to the silicic acid content.
Vascular tonic: Horsetail tea strengthens blood vessels, which can improve circulation and reduce sweating. This is due to the presence of rutin, a type of flavonoid.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 1 minute.
Steep the horsetail herb in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb.
Drink the horsetail tea, up to three times a day, to help reduce excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes are rich in silica and have astringent properties, making them useful for reducing excessive sweating.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain high amounts of silica and flavonoids, which can help control perspiration and soothe skin irritation caused by excessive sweating.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves are a natural source of tannins and essential oils, contributing to their ability to reduce excessive sweating and provide a calming effect on the body.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with excessive sweating because it contains antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate the body's natural cooling system. Fennel seeds, from which the tea is made, have been used traditionally to treat various health issues, including excessive perspiration. The tea is believed to help balance the body's internal thermostat, reducing the production of sweat. Fennel tea is also known for its calming effects, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety, two common triggers of excessive sweating. Drinking fennel tea regularly can help manage and minimize excessive sweating, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of volatile oils like anethole, can help reduce inflammation in the sweat glands, potentially decreasing excessive sweating.
Astringent: The astringent properties of fennel tea, caused by tannins, can help constrict sweat glands, reducing the amount of perspiration.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of fennel tea, from compounds like trans-anethole, can inhibit bacterial growth on the skin, reducing body odor associated with excessive sweating.
Diuretic: Fennel tea's diuretic properties, due to the presence of potassium and other minerals, can help eliminate excess fluid from the body, reducing overall perspiration.
Calming: Fennel tea's calming properties, from compounds like chamazulene, can help reduce stress and anxiety, both of which can trigger excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of crushed fennel seeds to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the fennel seeds.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for best results.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used in medicinal teas for excessive sweating due to their richness in flavonoids, which have astringent properties that can help reduce sweating.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used in tea for excessive sweating due to their high content of tannins and essential oils, which have a drying effect on the skin.
Leaves: Fennel leaves are used in medicinal teas for excessive sweating because they contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce sweating.
Flowers: Fennel flowers are used in medicinal teas for excessive sweating due to their high content of volatile oils, which have a calming effect on the body and can help regulate sweating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with excessive sweating because it contains an active compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to reduce sweat production. This compound works by inhibiting the sweat glands, thereby reducing the amount of sweat that is produced. Additionally, licorice tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing any discomfort or irritation that may be caused by excessive sweating. Furthermore, licorice tea is a natural and caffeine-free alternative to other beverages, making it a healthy and beneficial choice for those looking to manage excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, can help alleviate excessive sweating caused by inflammation or irritation in the body.

Adrenal gland support: The adrenal gland-supporting properties of licorice tea, attributed to its content of glycyrrhizic acid and flavonoids, can help regulate stress hormones, which can contribute to excessive perspiration.

Antimicrobial: Licorice tea's antimicrobial properties, resulting from compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, can help maintain skin health and reduce excessive sweating caused by skin infections or irritations.
Diuretic: Licorice tea's diuretic properties, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, can help regulate fluid balance in the body, reducing excessive sweating caused by fluid retention.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of licorice tea, attributed to its flavonoid content, can help protect cells from damage and support overall health, which can contribute to reduced excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried licorice root and place it in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes before pouring it over the licorice root.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Remove the tea infuser or strain out the licorice root.
Drink the tea up to three times a day for excessive sweating relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds that can help regulate sweat production, making them a popular choice for medicinal teas aimed at reducing excessive sweating.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including excessive sweating, due to their natural cooling and soothing properties.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain active compounds that can help regulate the body's fluid balance, which can be useful in reducing excessive sweating.

Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help reduce sweating and soothe irritated skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tea helps with excessive sweating because it contains compounds that can help regulate sweat gland activity. The plant has astringent properties, which can help to tighten and constrict pores, reducing the amount of sweat that is released. Additionally, English ivy has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin from irritation and infection that can occur as a result of excessive sweating. Drinking English ivy tea can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can be a contributing factor to excessive sweating. Overall, English ivy tea can be a natural and effective way to manage excessive sweating and promote healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Antiperspirant properties: English ivy contains saponins, which have been found to help reduce sweating and provide antiperspirant effects. This is important for excessive sweating as it helps to control the amount of sweat produced.

Astringent properties: English ivy has astringent properties due to its tannin content, which helps to tighten and constrict the skin's pores. This is crucial for excessive sweating as it can help minimize the size of pores and reduce sweat excretion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English ivy contains flavonoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory effects. This is significant for excessive sweating as it may help alleviate any skin irritation or inflammation caused by excessive perspiration.

Antimicrobial properties: English ivy possesses antimicrobial properties, attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. This is important for excessive sweating as it can help prevent bacterial growth and odor development resulting from sweat.

Vasoconstrictor properties: English ivy contains saponins and other compounds that can cause vasoconstriction, reducing blood flow in the skin. This is beneficial for excessive sweating as it can help decrease local blood flow and, consequently, reduce sweating.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 fresh English ivy leaves and rinse them thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and bacteria.
Chop the leaves finely and place them in a tea infuser or a reusable tea bag.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for 2-3 minutes before pouring it over the infuser or tea bag in a cup.
Steep the ivy leaves in hot water for 5-10 minutes, allowing the active compounds to extract.
Remove the infuser or tea bag and enjoy the herbal tea, preferably 2-3 times a day for excessive sweating relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of English ivy are commonly used in medicinal teas for excessive sweating due to their astringent properties, which help to tighten and constrict the skin's pores and reduce sweat production.

Stems: English ivy stems contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them useful in reducing inflammation and redness associated with excessive sweating.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of English ivy contain saponins, which have expectorant and diuretic properties, helping to eliminate toxins from the body and reduce fluid retention, which can contribute to excessive sweating.
Bark: The bark of English ivy contains tannins, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in reducing sweating and soothing irritated skin.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat excessive sweating with herbal teas is by regularly consuming sage and chamomile teas. These teas have antiperspirant and relaxant properties that help regulate sweat glands and reduce anxiety, a common cause of excessive sweating. Steep one teaspoon of dried herbs in boiling water for 10 minutes, strain and drink up to three cups a day. Remember, while herbal teas can be beneficial, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional for persistent or severe sweating issues.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to excessive sweating that are treated with herbal teas include anxiety and stress. Chamomile tea is known for its calming properties and can help reduce anxiety, which can in turn reduce excessive sweating. Peppermint tea is another herbal tea that can help alleviate symptoms of excessive sweating as it has a cooling effect on the body and can help regulate body temperature. Additionally, ginger tea can help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, which can also help reduce sweating.
